data.title: As a developer, I have validated that @playwright/cli meets DSO's browser automation needs
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Spike task to validate @playwright/cli@0.1.1 capabilities before committing to full migration across 5 skills. Validate run-code as browser_wait_for substitute (including async DOM mutation patterns), confirm browser binary sharing with existing Python Playwright installation (including Chromium revision compatibility), verify session persistence across discrete Bash invocations, and confirm output format compatibility.
**Why**: The feasibility review identified unverified assumptions that block implementation: run-code async behavior, binary sharing, and alpha-stage dependency risks. Validating these in a spike prevents wasted effort on an unviable migration path.
**Scope**:
- IN: Proof-of-concept scripts testing run-code with async waits, session persistence across multiple Bash calls, binary sharing verification, Chromium revision comparison, output format inspection for snapshot/screenshot/console commands
- OUT: Migrating any production skill; writing the CLI guide; cleanup script changes

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, a proof-of-concept demonstrates that @playwright/cli run-code can wait for async DOM mutations (page.waitForSelector, page.waitForLoadState) and exits within 60 seconds on a test page
  ← Satisfies: "Each browser interaction command completes within the ~73s Bash tool timeout"
- When this story is complete, browser binaries are confirmed shared between @playwright/cli and the existing Python Playwright installation (same Chromium revision, no duplicate download required)
  ← Satisfies: "All 5 skills use @playwright/cli commands via Bash"
- When this story is complete, session persistence is confirmed: a navigate command followed by a separate click command (different Bash invocations, same -s=name session) operates on the same page state
  ← Satisfies: "Sub-agents dispatched via the Agent tool can perform browser automation without MCP access"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] run-code executes JS synchronously — async mutations after return may cause stale reads; test with setTimeout and MutationObserver patterns
- [Reliability] @0.1.1 is alpha with no stability guarantee — pin version, document fallback
- [Reliability] Chromium revision mismatch between Python and CLI Playwright is the primary binary sharing failure mode
